acceptance: 1. ddx bead status shows child beads for all object-log implementation-plan milestones. 2. Every child bead has a spec-id pointing at the governing HELIX artifact. 3. This epic closes only after all child beads are closed.
description: Epic for taking object-log from the current core prototype through production-ready reusable log library scope. Governing docs: docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md, docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-001-core-and-object-backend.md, TD-002-s3-adapter-retention-and-snapshots.md, TD-003-conformance-kafka-backend-and-extraction.md, and docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md. In scope: backend conformance, pqueue/Niflheim fixtures, snapshot/retention, S3-compatible adapter, Kafka backend adapter, and extraction readiness. Out of scope: Fjord Kafka wire protocol and product-specific pqueue/Niflheim schemas.
title: Complete object-log full-scope delivery

acceptance: 1. cargo test passes. 2. Tests prove failed retention CAS does not delete segment objects. 3. Tests prove orphan cleanup only removes unreferenced segments older than the configured safe age.
description: Implement retention execution for memory/local object backends. In-scope files: src/object_backend.rs, src/store.rs, retention module files, and tests. Add CAS-protected manifest rewrite that marks retired segments before deleting objects, and orphan cleanup for segment objects written by failed manifest CAS attempts. Out of scope: S3 SDK adapter and downstream projection snapshots.
title: Implement CAS-protected retention and orphan cleanup

acceptance: 1. cargo test passes. 2. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes. 3. Shared conformance tests run against both MemoryObjectStore and LocalObjectStore object backends.
description: Refactor object-log tests so all LogBackend implementations run through one shared conformance suite. In-scope files: tests/object_backend.rs and new tests/support or src test helper modules as needed. Cover append/read ordering, ack modes, idempotent producer retry, stale epoch, CAS conflict, corruption, and opaque pqueue/Niflheim payload round trips. Out of scope: adding S3 SDK or Kafka backend implementations.
title: Extract object-log backend conformance suite

acceptance: 1. cargo test passes. 2. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes. 3. Configuration rejects production use when manifest CAS cannot be provided natively or by a configured delegated CAS provider. 4. Local S3-compatible integration tests pass when the documented test endpoint is available.
description: Add a runtime-configured S3-compatible ObjectStore adapter. In-scope files: object store adapter module, configuration types, provider capability validation, and local S3-compatible integration tests. The adapter is selected by configuration, not compile-time feature flags. Out of scope: hosted control plane, pqueue projection code, and Fjord Kafka protocol.
title: Add configurable S3-compatible object store adapter

acceptance: 1. New docs map pqueue and Niflheim requirements to object-log APIs and conformance cases. 2. Docs explicitly state backend selection is runtime configuration, not feature flags. 3. ddx doc stale reports no stale HELIX documents or any staleness is documented as unrelated/tooling.
description: Create downstream integration maps for pqueue and Niflheim. In-scope files: docs/helix integration or migration docs under docs/helix/02-design or docs/helix/04-build. Capture how pqueue uses object-log for command log plus SQLite projection snapshots, how Niflheim can extract object WAL primitives, and where each can swap between S3/object-log and Kafka backend by configuration. Out of scope: modifying pqueue or Niflheim code.
title: Document pqueue and Niflheim object-log migration maps

acceptance: 1. cargo test passes. 2. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes. 3. Adapter maps append/read to Kafka produce/fetch semantics and passes the shared LogBackend conformance suite where an integration Kafka broker is configured. 4. Without an integration broker, unit tests still validate configuration and error mapping.
description: Add a runtime-configured Kafka-backed LogBackend adapter so pqueue and Niflheim can choose a real Kafka-compatible broker for lower commit latency while keeping the object-log projection-facing contract. In-scope files: Kafka adapter module, configuration types, error normalization, and env-gated real Kafka integration tests. Runtime config selects this backend; do not add compile-time feature flags for backend selection. Out of scope: Kafka TCP server implementation and consumer group coordination.
title: Add configurable Kafka LogBackend adapter

acceptance: 1. cargo test retention passes or cargo test passes with retention tests included. 2. Retention planning keeps all segments after the snapshot high watermark plus safety margin. 3. Retention planning reports blockers and performs no object deletion.
description: Add object-log snapshot and retention model types from TD-002. In-scope files: src/model.rs or a new src/retention.rs, src/lib.rs exports, and unit tests. Implement pure retention planning for manifests without deleting objects. Out of scope: S3 adapter, object deletion, pqueue SQLite snapshots, and Niflheim row snapshots.
title: Add snapshot and retention planning model
